project.title: Apple Music and Spotify Persona Companion
project.team: Gabriel Bankhead, Matt Rushforth, Ashton Osazuwa, Benjamin Todd
project.summary: This agent supports a printed music research exhibit centered on persona work and an Apple Music journey map. It helps visitors ask follow-up questions about the personas, compare Apple Music and Spotify users, and understand what the journey map reveals about behavior, friction, trust, and retention.
how_to_answer: Ground every answer in the uploaded persona and journey-map material.
Do not claim the project studied people or behaviors that are not present in the provided files.
If a visitor asks for something not shown in the files, say that the current materials do not include it.
When useful, compare Apple Music and Spotify personas by motivations, friction, and platform fit.
Keep answers clear, concise, and welcoming for someone scanning a QR code from a printed artifact.
assignments_overview: The materials present a set of music-user personas across Apple Music and Spotify.
The main journey-map artifact focuses on Apple Music and overlays two Apple Music personas: Reid Madsen and Sheila Applegate.
The journey map tracks emotional shifts and friction across ten stages, from platform relationship to long-term takeaway.
available_material_notes: The provided Personas PDF contains five personas total: three Spotify personas and two Apple Music personas.
The provided Journey Map PDF contains one Apple Music user journey map with overlaid persona comparison.
If the team has additional persona files beyond these PDFs, they are not yet loaded into this knowledge base.

journey_map_overview.focus: Apple Music user journey map with overlaid persona comparison for Reid Madsen, 'The Audiophile,' and Sheila Applegate, 'The Apple Loyalist.'
journey_map_overview.audience_scale_note: The artifact labels Apple Music as having 108 million users.
key_takeaways: Apple Music works best when it feels deeply integrated with the Apple ecosystem and preserves quality across devices.
Trust is a major theme: reliability and continuity build confidence, while inconsistency causes a sharp emotional drop.
Audio quality is central to the Apple Music experience presented here, especially for the audiophile persona.
Friction often appears during cross-device transitions, hidden settings, library complexity, and moments where quality feels inconsistent.

stages.label: Platform Relationship
stages.note: High baseline because audio quality matters immediately.

stages.label: Content Selection
stages.note: More invested when format choices feel rich.

stages.label: Maintenance Over Time
stages.note: Control issues and complexity drag the feeling down.

stages.label: Platform Relationship
stages.note: Starts with trust because the ecosystem already feels familiar.

stages.label: Trigger Moment
stages.note: Comfort builds when the system feels familiar.

stages.label: Cross Context Use
stages.note: Cross-device flow still feels dependable here.

stages.label: Success State
stages.note: Trust starts to recover once things feel stable again.

goal: Play music that fits the moment

thoughts: "This should continue where I left off"
"How do I get the best output?"

goal: Choose music for context and audience

insights: Quality failure leads to a sharp drop

frictions: Stops support if trust is lost

apple_music.name: Reid Madsen
apple_music.short_bio: Loves enjoying music on high-quality equipment and uses music while programming or gaming.
goals: Get the highest audio quality possible
Access a wide range of artists
Use a platform that supports critical listening
behaviors: Gets annoyed with sub-par audio
Keeps up with state-of-the-art equipment
Nerds out about the science and technology
Is willing to pay for high-quality audio
Loves niche old-school artists
pain_points: Needs a high-quality platform with a wide range of artists
motivations: Access to artists without extra distractions
apple_music.quote: The materials do not include a direct first-person quote for Reid.

journey_notes.discovery: Evaluates Apple Music in terms of quality and ecosystem fit.
journey_notes.onboarding: Likely sensitive to whether quality controls are visible and understandable.
journey_notes.daily_use: Listens critically and notices compression, inconsistency, or handoff issues.
journey_notes.friction: Quality inconsistency, hidden settings, and playback inconsistency strongly affect trust.
journey_notes.retention_or_switching: Likely to stay when quality is validated and leave if trust in fidelity drops.

apple_music.name: Sheila Applegate
apple_music.short_bio: Picked up iTunes early, follows Apple closely, and sees Apple Music as a natural extension of her long-term investment in the Apple ecosystem.
goals: Build an environment that is comfortable for her family
Stay up to date with all things Apple
Connect music with her home, car, and everyday life
behaviors: Enjoys the interconnected Apple ecosystem
Pays for the Apple Music family plan
Listens through her smart home system
Buys the latest Apple products and stays updated
Exercises to music
pain_points: Finding how to upload music from iTunes
why_this_platform: Fits well into what she knows
apple_music.quote: The materials do not include a direct first-person quote for Sheila.

journey_notes.discovery: Apple Music feels like an easy extension of her existing Apple habits.
journey_notes.onboarding: Benefits from familiarity but can be thrown by updates or legacy iTunes tasks.
journey_notes.daily_use: Uses Apple Music across home systems, family settings, and exercise routines.
journey_notes.friction: Update changes, family-plan complexity, and confusion around older iTunes workflows.
journey_notes.retention_or_switching: Likely retained by ecosystem value, ease, and continuity across devices.

spotify.name: Franklin Jacobs
spotify.job: Lead Associate at a popular retail clothing store
spotify.short_bio: Uses music to connect with others by sharing playlists and discussing new releases with friends and family.
goals: Stay up to date with trends
Be seen as someone with good taste
behaviors: Curates playlists for work shifts and social settings
Starts and joins Jam Sessions
Shares and collaborates on playlists
Actively seeks recommendations and new music
Attends concerts and music festivals
spotify.quote: The materials do not include a direct first-person quote for Franklin.

journey_notes.discovery: Responds well to social recommendation and collaboration features.
journey_notes.onboarding: Likes a platform that quickly exposes social tools and shared listening.
journey_notes.daily_use: Playlist making, collaborative listening, and social sharing are core habits.
journey_notes.friction: Feels let down when interaction is passive or isolating.
journey_notes.retention_or_switching: Most loyal when the platform makes music feel social and participatory.

spotify.name: Brayden Johnson
spotify.short_bio: Uses Spotify to learn about the world and the news through podcasts and enjoys being part of podcast communities.
goals: Learn more about the news through podcasts
Stay up to date with favorite podcasters
behaviors: Loves listening to podcasts whenever he can
Wants to stay up to date with the world
Uses audio to lock in
Mainly listens to podcasts but sometimes also music
pain_points: Does not know when new podcast episodes come out
Does not have anyone to discuss episodes with
Wants to find podcasts similar to what he already likes
why_this_platform: It is the same app as his music
spotify.quote: The materials do not include a direct first-person quote for Brayden.

journey_notes.discovery: Finds value in Spotify as a combined music and podcast platform.
journey_notes.onboarding: Likely wants better episode surfacing and community hooks.
journey_notes.daily_use: Podcast-heavy use with occasional music listening.
journey_notes.friction: Discovery and community discussion are weaker than he wants.
journey_notes.retention_or_switching: Stays when new episodes and adjacent podcast recommendations are easy to track.

spotify.name: Brandon Clark
spotify.short_bio: Primarily listens during commutes, chores, and everyday routines, usually letting playlists run in the background.
goals: Add background music to mundane tasks
behaviors: Uses Daily Mixes and Daily Drive regularly
Rarely searches for new artists intentionally
Mostly listens to a rotation of favorite artists
Likes to play music around the house
Lets playlists run without adjusting often
pain_points: Having to actively search for music
why_this_platform: Able to make playlists for free
The algorithm does the work for him
spotify.quote: The materials do not include a direct first-person quote for Brandon.

journey_notes.discovery: Benefits from familiar, low-effort recommendation systems.
journey_notes.onboarding: Needs a simple experience that starts working quickly.
journey_notes.daily_use: Passive listening across chores, commuting, and home routines.
journey_notes.friction: Ads and repetitive recommendations disrupt what should be effortless listening.
journey_notes.retention_or_switching: Likely stays when the platform remains easy, familiar, and minimally demanding.

comparative_insights: The Spotify personas in these materials emphasize social connection, podcasts, passive listening, and convenience.
The Apple Music personas emphasize ecosystem fit, family integration, continuity across devices, and especially audio quality.
Franklin values collaboration and group listening, while Sheila values family sharing and ecosystem familiarity.
Brandon wants low-effort background listening, while Reid is highly attentive to fidelity and control.
The Apple Music journey map shows trust and quality consistency as especially important to long-term retention.
faq_seed_questions: How do the Apple Music personas differ from the Spotify personas in these materials?
Which persona is most motivated by social connection?
Which persona cares most about audio quality, and how does that shape the journey?
Where does the Apple Music journey map show the biggest friction points?
What does the journey map suggest Apple Music should improve over time?
